sanskrit_devanagari:
श्रीभगवानुवाच
काम एष क्रोध एष रजोगुणसमुद्भवः ।
महाशनो महापाप्मा विद्ध्येनमिह वैरिणम् ॥ ३७ ॥
sanskrit_iast:
śrī-bhagavān uvāca
kāma eṣa krodha eṣa
rajo-guṇa-samudbhavaḥ |
mahāśano mahā-pāpmā
viddhy enam iha vairiṇam || 37 ||

sanskrit_sources.agreement: exact (raw HTML scrape; akṣara sequence as published)
exact (text body identical with vedabase; punctuation rendering differs)
exact (text body identical; punctuation rendering differs)
exact (academic critical edition; cross-reference Bhg_03.037 = MBh_06,025.037). Body text identical; word-segmentation differs by edition.

translations.verbatim_quote: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: It is lust only, Arjuna, which is born of contact with the material mode of passion and later transformed into wrath, and which is the all-devouring sinful enemy of this world.
(See holy-bhagavad-gita.org for full Mukundananda rendering; agreement with Prabhupada noted in disagreements section where it diverges materially.)

commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: When a living entity comes in contact with the material creation, his eternal love for Kṛṣṇa is transformed into lust, in association with the mode of passion. Or, in other words, the sense of love of God becomes transformed into lust, as milk in contact with sour tamarind is transformed into yogurt.

commentaries.translator: Swami Gambhirananda (Advaita Ashrama edition of Sankara-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 3.37 Esah, this; kamah, desire, is the enemy of the whole world, because of which the creatures incur all evil. This desire when obstructed in any way turns into anger. Therefore, krodhah, anger, is also identical with this (desire).
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Shankara: śrī-bhagavān uvāca — the Blessed Lord said. kāma eṣa krodha eṣa — this is desire, this is anger. rajo-guṇa-samudbhavaḥ — born of the mode of passion. mahāśanaḥ — all-devouring. mahā-pāpmā — very sinful. viddhi enam iha vairiṇam — know this to be the enemy in this world. The verse identifies the agent of 3.36's experienced compulsion: kāma in its desire-form, krodha as its frustrated form, both products of rajas.

commentaries.translator: Swami Adidevananda (Sri Ramakrishna Math edition of Ramanuja's Gita-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 3.37 The Lord said — The highly ravenous desire is born of the Guna Rajas originating from old subtle impressions. It has for its objects sound and other sense contacts.
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Ramanuja: the verse names kāma — the deep-seated craving for sense-pleasures — and its transform krodha — anger when the craving is frustrated. Both are born of rajo-guṇa. Together they constitute the single iha-vairin — the enemy in this world — that drives the actor toward sin against his own explicit will. The rest of the chapter analyses this single enemy.

positions.rendering: it is lust only, Arjuna, which is born of contact with the material mode of passion and later transformed into wrath, and which is the all-devouring sinful enemy of this world
it is desire alone, which is born of contact with the mode of passion, and later transformed into anger. Know this as the sinful, all-devouring enemy in the world

kāma = desire for sense-objects; krodha = the anger that arises when desire is obstructed; both are rajo-guṇa-samudbhavaḥ — born of the mode of passion. They are paired as cause and consequence in the same single agent
kāma = the deep-seated craving for sense-pleasures; krodha = its anger-form when frustrated; the verse identifies them as the single enemy whose two faces produce the experienced compulsion of 3.36

disagreements_among_translators.explanation: Translators (especially Prabhupada) sometimes render kāma as 'lust' which narrows the term to sexual desire; the technical reading is broader — kāma is the desire-faculty as such, of which sexual desire is one instance. Krodha is its frustration-transform. Both commentators stress the single-enemy reading: kāma and krodha are not two separate enemies but one agent in two phases.

literal_meaning: The Blessed Lord said: It is kāma, it is krodha, born of rajo-guṇa — all-devouring, very sinful — know this to be the enemy in this world.
traditional_meaning_consensus: Krishna's answer to Arjuna's question of 3.36. The agent of forced sinful action is kāma — desire — and its transform krodha — anger. Both are born of rajo-guṇa, the mode of passion. They are mahāśanaḥ (all-devouring) and mahā-pāpmā (very sinful). The verse names this as the iha-vairin — the enemy in this world. Sets the diagnostic frame for the rest of the chapter.
